g.text: • Central finite difference: perturb ±Δ, evaluate forward model
• Jacobian of wrench W w.r.t. force inputs [F₁,s₁,θ₁,F₂,s₂,θ₂]
• η = sensor noise std. dev. (per output channel)
• R = operating range of each force input [F₁,s₁,θ₁,F₂,s₂,θ₂]
SVD(Jw) = UΣVT → σmin = smallest singular value ("weakest direction")
• σmin<1: unobservable | σmin>1: detectable | σmin≈1: threshold
• Since Jw is noise-normalized → σmin directly interpretable as SNR
• Sigmoid focuses gradient on borderline cases (σmin≈τ) where design changes matter
• Avoids wasting effort on easy (σmin≫τ) or impossible (σmin≪τ) cases
Identify worst-case load via moment estimator:
Run forward model → internal wrenches → Von Mises stress per link
Factor of Safety (must be ≥ 1 for all links):
• Transverse Forces: Applied perpendicular to local axis
• Concentric Forces: Pass through geometric center
• Follower Forces: Defined relative to local frame
• Elastic Deformation: Purely elastic with full recovery
• Computes the proximal wrench W required to hold quasi-static equilibrium
configuration under the applied load and tendon tensions
Return: W = [Tx, Ty, Tz, Fx, Fy, Fz]
